ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class; no specific lemon green tea with reduced sugar subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers ready-to-drink green tea; no specific lemon-flavoured reduced-sugar variant code exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for lemon green tea beverage identified in publicly available SNOMED CT browser; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard takeaway cup of lemon green tea (less sugar) is approximately 250 ml (250 g), as commonly served at Singaporean hawker stalls and bubble tea shops.

nutrient_source.title: Lemon Green Tea (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo database entry for Lemon green tea (less sugar), food code D08010064, used directly. All per-100g values sourced from this entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for lemon green tea (less sugar) exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low, per standard GI classification: low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70) based on the GI of sucrose (~65) and diluted sugar-sweetened tea beverages, weighted by the sugar concentration of 8.26g/100g. The beverage is predominantly water with dissolved sucrose; GI of sugar-sweetened beverages in the Sydney database ranges 58–65 for cola-type drinks and ~50–60 for diluted sugar solutions. A conservative midpoint of 55 is applied, placing this beverage at the upper boundary of the low GI category. Glycemic load per 250g serving = (55 × 8.26 × 2.5) / 100 = 11.4g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.26g sugar/100g independently raises blood pressure risk; sodium is negligible at 1mg/100g
hypertension.guidance: Despite very low sodium (1mg/100g), the 8.26g sugar/100g classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage that independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines; individuals with hypertension should request genuinely unsweetened lemon green tea (zero added sugar).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.26g sugar/100g and negligible dietary fibre (0.03g/100g); liquid sugar raises postprandial glucose rapidly
type2_diabetes.guidance: At 8.26g sugar/100g with virtually zero dietary fibre, this beverage triggers the ADA sugar-sweetened beverage hard gate for Type 2 Diabetes; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid all sweetened tea variants and choose only genuinely unsweetened (0g sugar) lemon green tea.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages at this sugar concentration is associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk via dyslipidaemia and insulin resistance pathways
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The 8.26g sugar/100g in this beverage, consumed regularly, contributes to cardiovascular risk through elevated triglycerides and insulin resistance; individuals managing cardiovascular disease should limit sugar-sweetened beverages and opt for unsweetened green tea, which provides EGCG polyphenols without the sugar burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (8.26g/100g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators, negating the EGCG anti-inflammatory benefit of the green tea base
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The 8.26g added sugar/100g promotes inflammatory cytokine activity and negates the EGCG polyphenol anti-inflammatory benefit of the green tea base; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should choose genuinely unsweetened lemon green tea to benefit from EGCG and vitamin C anti-inflammatory properties.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lemon Green Tea (Less Sugar) is a popular Singaporean ready-to-drink cold tea beverage made from freshly brewed green tea combined with lemon juice or lemon slices and a reduced quantity of added sugar. It is widely available at hawker centres, kopitiam stalls, and bubble tea shops across Singapore. The beverage has a light, slightly astringent green tea flavour balanced by the tartness of lemon and a mild sweetness from the reduced sugar content. The texture is thin and watery, similar to a diluted sweetened tea. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the beverage still contains 8.26g of sugar per 100g (approximately 20.7g per 250ml serving), which classifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under both ADA and AHA clinical guidelines. The energy content is 43 kcal per 100g (approximately 107.5 kcal per 250ml serving). Protein is negligible at 0.01g/100g and fat is effectively zero, making carbohydrates (entirely from sugar) the sole macronutrient of significance. Dietary fibre is negligible at 0.03g/100g. Sodium is very low at 1mg/100g. The green tea base provides trace amounts of EGCG catechins and antioxidants, but the clinical benefit of these polyphenols is substantially offset by the added sugar content in this sweetened form. Individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage and choose genuinely unsweetened lemon green tea instead. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low GI, upper boundary) based on the sugar concentration and analogous sugar-sweetened beverage GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database.

confidence_reason: Exact HPB Foodo database entry for this food item (D08010064) used directly with confidence 0.95 as reported by the source. All per-100g macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced from the HPB Singapore canonical database. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence medium) as no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database (D08010064); this is consistent with a brewed tea beverage containing no fat-bearing ingredients. Saturated_fat_g and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero by the same rationale. Protein_g is trace at 0.01g/100g (not zero). GI category corrected to 'low' (GI ≤55) from previous draft value of 'medium'; a GI of 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low GI category per standard University of Sydney GI classification (low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70). Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, full vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific lemon green tea (less sugar) formulation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry D08010064 as of 2026. Calcium_mg (1.0mg/100g) and iron_mg (0.07mg/100g) are reported by HPB. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sugar-sweetened beverages and diluted sucrose solutions; no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. Glycemic load per 250g serving calculated as (55 × 8.26 × 2.5) / 100 = 11.4g. The 'less sugar' label does not indicate zero sugar; this product remains a sugar-sweetened beverage at 8.26g sugar/100g and is clinically unsuitable for diabetes and hypertension management.
